title: ArticleListView: core structure and initial load
description: Create AggregatorApp/Articles/ArticleListView.swift. State: articles:[Article], nextCursor:String?, sort:ArticleSort (default .importance), unreadOnly:Bool (default false), a loading phase enum (loading/loaded/error), and isLoadingMore:Bool. On appear: fetch first page via APIClient.getArticles with limit:15; show a centered ProgressView while in-flight. On success: transition to loaded, render rows. Each row is ArticleRowView inside a NavigationLink to ArticlePagerView(articles: articles, startIndex: i). Empty state: ContentUnavailableView with appropriate message. Error state: message + Retry button. Treat isCancellation errors as benign — do not transition to error state for cancelled fetches (use the existing isCancellation helper). Apply .navigationTitle(feed.title). Run xcodegen generate after creating the file.
acceptance_criteria: Centered ProgressView shown during initial load
Rows render via ArticleRowView in a List
NavigationLink to ArticlePagerView passes correct articles array and startIndex
Error state shows message + Retry; cancelled fetches produce no error state
File compiles without errors after xcodegen generate

handoff_summary.completed: ArticleListView.swift created with all acceptance criteria met: loading spinner, first-page limit:15, ArticleRowView rows in List, NavigationLink to ArticlePagerView with correct articles+startIndex, ContentUnavailableView empty state, error+Retry state, isCancellation guard, navigationTitle from feed.title, and successful compile after xcodegen generate.
handoff_summary.remaining: None for this bead. The view intentionally does not persist sort/unreadOnly preferences across launches (no spec requirement for that); a follow-up bead could add that if needed.
handoff_summary.risks: ArticleListView must be embedded in a NavigationStack by its caller — it does not own one itself (matching how ThreadDetailView and similar child views work). The caller must provide the NavigationStack and appropriate tab integration.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify: ProgressView appears during load, rows render correctly from ArticleRowView, NavigationLink opens ArticlePagerView at correct startIndex, empty feed shows ContentUnavailableView, a simulated error shows the Retry button, cancellation does not transition to error state.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — ArticleListView.swift is a new file with no overlap with any sibling bead's expected_files.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Modeled directly on ThreadsView.swift to maintain codebase consistency. Key choices: (1) Sort and unreadOnly are local @State rather than persisted preferences — the spec does not require persistence and no ListPreferences entry exists for articles yet. (2) NavigationLink(value: index) + .navigationDestination(for: Int.self) used to pass the full articles array and startIndex to ArticlePagerView, consistent with how ThreadPagerView is navigated in ThreadsView. (3) The view does not own a NavigationStack — it is designed to be pushed onto a caller's stack, keeping nav ownership at the tab level. (4) unreadOnly toggle is conditionally shown via feed.allowsUnreadFilter, which returns false for the .unread feed (it would be redundant there).
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should cover: (a) Loading phase — ProgressView is centered while fetch is in flight. (b) Loaded phase — rows appear, each NavigationLink opens ArticlePagerView with articles array and correct startIndex. (c) Empty state — ContentUnavailableView shown when response has empty items. (d) Error state — error message and Retry button shown; tapping Retry re-triggers loadFirstPage. (e) Cancellation — simulating task cancellation must NOT transition to error state. (f) Sort picker — changing sort from importance to recent reloads from first page. (g) Unread filter — toggling unreadOnly reloads; toggle absent for .unread feed. (h) Infinite scroll — scrolling to last row triggers loadNextPage and appends results. (i) Pull-to-refresh — .refreshable reloads without showing spinner.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Sort and unreadOnly preferences are not persisted across app launches. If persistence is required, a follow-up bead should extend ListPreferences with articlesSort and articlesUnreadOnly fields. No toolbar is added for the .unread feed since allowsUnreadFilter=false hides the unreadOnly toggle — the sort picker is still present for all feeds.

conflict_risks: None — ArticleListView.swift is a new file with no overlap with any sibling bead's expected_files.

execution_history.summary: Created AggregatorApp/Articles/ArticleListView.swift implementing the full article list screen. The view follows the established ThreadsView pattern: private LoadPhase enum (loading/loaded/error), @Environment(CredentialsStore.self) injection, computed APIClient property, limit:15 first-page fetch on .task, GlassEffectContainer+List for the loaded state, NavigationLink(value: index) with .navigationDestination to ArticlePagerView, ContentUnavailableView for empty state, error+Retry UI, isCancellation guard, cursor-based next-page loading triggered by last-row .onAppear, and .navigationTitle(feed.title). xcodegen regenerated and xcodebuild compiled cleanly.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
